title: Aedh Hears The Cry Of The Sedge
content:
I Wander by the edge
Of this desolate lake
Where wind cries in the sedge
Until the axle break
That keeps the stars in their round
And hands hurl in the deep
The banners of East and West
And the girdle of light is unbound,
Your breast will not lie by the breast
Of your beloved in sleep.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Butler Yeats delivers a powerful performance in "Aedh Hears The Cry Of The Sedge"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
